content.text:
Build the next great interaction design to shape culture as we know it, using Figma Make.

Makeathon participants will receive access to Figma Make for the duration of the hackathon. After joining, check your confirmation email from Contra and click the access code link to unlock Figma Make.
Your submission should highlight how Figma Make, paired with your taste and creativity, can unlock ideas that shape culture.
Be built using Figma Make during the official challenge window
] Be submitted as a published Figma Make project - you’ll see “
” at the end of your published URL once published
Share the editable Figma Make file with
May include a link to the site posted on social for extra points
May include a link to the Figma Community published file for extra points
The winning submissions will be selected based on one or more of the following criteria:
Creative use of Figma Make to create a new interaction and working prototype
Project that pushes the boundaries of digital creation and innovation using Figma Make
Re-imagination of an iconic design interaction (swipe left/right, multiplayer collaboration, see more
Clarity, intentionality, and craft of design interaction
Smoothness and responsiveness of interactions across UI
Quality of design craft and polish
Out-of-the-box thinking and innovation using Figma Make
Originality and creativity of the prototype
$50,000 — Best Overall: Most creative and innovative use of Figma Make to create a design interaction that shapes culture as we know it
You are required to also post your submission to socials - Instagram, X or Linkedin (tag
. Once you submit, you will be prompted to share your social links on Contra.
Here is what makes a great social post:
The video can include yourself, a voiceover or just a screen recording
1-2 sentences about your creation and how you built it
Tag the tool you built with

portfolioProject.description:
My entry for the Figma Makeathon: Procedural Spring.
A procedural tree generator with default styles based on Figma colors and additional customization optio...

content.text: My entry for the Figma Makeathon: Procedural Spring.
A procedural tree generator with default styles based on Figma colors and additional customization options.
You can control growth, sway, droop, and bloom through the camera or manually. Move to interact or fine tune every parameter yourself.
Minimal and slightly cozy. Hope you enjoy it!
The constellation wouldn't be the same without you, Ismel. You're in there because your work deserved to be. Means a lot that you found Contra's VIBE MATCH & TRUST SCORE valuable. 🙏
Thanks so much dude. I'm really excited to see what's next for this project 🔥

Really impressive — camera-based interaction inside Figma Make is a leap. Curious how you handled gesture recognition latency and feedback loops? The micro-response timing probably makes or breaks the experience

Totally agree, perceived responsiveness is key. I focused on stabilizing detection and adding instant micro-feedback to keep the loop feeling tight. 
Still exploring ways to refine the interaction window.

webPageMetadata.title: Gesture Tower Game Development
webPageMetadata.description: Engage in a fun physics-based game where players use hand gestures to stack ovals and build towers, enhancing coordination and reflexes.

content.text: Grab the Bears - A Camera-Based Gesture Interaction
A few years ago, gesture interaction required a full engineering team and months of development.
For the Figma Makeathon, I built a working camera-controlled gesture game on my own - entirely in Figma Make.
Grab the Bears reimagines tap and swipe as physical gestures. Players use their right hand to grab floating bears in real time.

• Camera-driven embodied interaction
• Real-time gesture detection
• Animated UI + sound micro-interactions
• Dynamic result generation with shareable export?

webPageMetadata.description: Explore and practice traditional tatreez embroidery patterns through an interactive platform that educates users on their origins and techniques.

content.text: Improving the Custom Design feature in my #FigmaMakeathon project “Tatreez Lab.”
You can now upload any reference image, trace or stitch the design directly on the canvas, and instantly preview it on real product mockups.

The goal was to make the experience more fluid and creative — turning the platform into a space where anyone can experiment, design freely, and see their ideas come to life.

Try it:

portfolioProject.description:
Imagine conducting ambient music in mid-air. It's like Minority Report, but instead of solving crimes you’re just gently adjusting your nervous system.
For #...

content.text: Imagine conducting ambient music in mid-air. It's like Minority Report, but instead of solving crimes you’re just gently adjusting your nervous system.
For #FigmaMakeathon I built Airwwave (AR) in #FigmaMake: a fullscreen performance instrument where gestures become live ambient soundscapes. It’s built to feel like conducting, not clicking.
Video is long because it’s a performance piece. I’m still hunting bugs, improving responsiveness, and cross browser performance until deadline.
Features: 28 ambient loops (hosted on Cloudinary), 12s fade in/out, 7-step LCD onboarding with 0–100% hold bar, 2-hand tracking + skeleton, 28 pads.
Gestures/FX: select + trigger, speed, reverb, octave, lock, stereo width + pan, tremolo/echo motion, filter resonance, volume shaping.

webPageMetadata.title: I built a drum machine with no audio files. It exports your beat, here's how: For the Figma Makeathon at Contra, I built: UnBeatable, an AI-powered drum machine prototype using Figma Make. A… | Ismel Figueroa
webPageMetadata.description:
I built a drum machine with no audio files. It exports your beat, here’s how: For the Figma Makeathon at Contra, I built: UnBeatable, an AI-powered drum machine prototype using Figma Make. A working music creation tool. Here is what makes it different from every other “interactive prototype” you have seen. Most prototypes stop at the interaction. Tap a button. Something happens. You leave with nothing. UnBeatable changes that...you leave with your downloadable beat. Here’s 7 things I built into his project: - Fully synthesized sound engine, no audio files.
- Glitch-free playback A dual setInterval and request
- Audio export Render your pattern and export your beat.
- AI beat generation using Claude/Supabase and API Credits.
- Full dark and light mode with smooth 0.25s palette transitions.
- 9 genre presets like Rock, Hip Hop, Electronic, Reggae, Latin, etc.
- Every control a real musician would expect, built into the prototype layer. Most designers prototype to show a concept. The best designers prototype to ship a capability. When your prototype can produce real output, audio, data, a file, it stops being a demo and starts being a product argument. That is a completely different conversation to have with a client or a team. UnBeatable is proof that Figma Make can go further than most people think. What you build in the prototype layer does not have to stay there? P.S. If you want to see the full build, interaction system, synthesis engine, and AI integration, here’s the free domain link: https://lnkd.in/gUP_WvyY

webPageMetadata.title: UnBeatable
webPageMetadata.description: Create custom drum beats effortlessly by typing your desired rhythm, then fine-tune with an interactive interface for musicians and producers.

content.text: : An AI-Powered Drum Machine with Real-Time Synthesis & Audio Export

UnBeatable is a fully functional drum machine prototype built with Figma Make that synthesizes sound in real time

It integrates Claude AI for beat generation, and enable you to export your beats as audio 🔊

This project allowed me to explore how Figma Make can really go from design surface to programmable creation tool

-
Available to Remix at Figma Community

Social link in the comments below
🥁

Figma make uses opus and sonnet and gemini. They can all handle game logic using the canvas element. However the art can't be done through them because figma make doesn't allow it.  If draw it yourself then all good.

content.text:
😡😡 Figma Make CANNOT create a beautiful serious game!

I'm here to prove the opposite!
Hi guys, I'm Howard Le, a Game UXUI Designer. This is my 1st time using Figma Make, but I'm starting to love it immediately!

All of the UI design assets were made by me!
- Concept background art
- HUD, Cards, Diegetic UI, Spatial UI, Meta UI, Non-Meta UI.
- VFX, Animation
- And ofc, I've asked Figma Make to turn it into the real things by my stupid, dumb prompt.

I hope this game could help Figma Make pay more attention about Gaming UI Industry!

You can try my game (Desktop version) at:

webPageMetadata.title: ssshtill — Find calm and connection through touch
webPageMetadata.description: Experience soothing relief from pain, anxiety, and sleeplessness with rhythmic haptic feedback, calming audio, and gentle visuals tailored for relaxation.

content.text: We built ssshtill because the world is losing touch. Not metaphorically. Literally.
We reverse-engineered ssshtill from the most human starting point — the feeling of touch.

Solo mode uses haptic pulses and ambient visuals for anxiety, pain, and sleep. 

Shared mode connects two devices in real time through synchronized breath, touch, and rhythm.
Figma Make let us prototype touch, timing, and sync as core mechanics — not afterthoughts. Emotion became interaction.
NOTE: Haptic is not available on iOS web browsers. ssshtill is best experienced on mobile devices.

content.text: Introducing Spacecraft : A fun creative & collaboration project by

& Me for the #FigmaMakeathon 

Unleash your imagination with the power of your fingers. Turn on your webcam and get started with the experience. Draw anything. Fill it with color. Experiment with brushes. Play without limits. 

Use your index finger to draw, Use 2 fingers to erase and then use tools like fill, different brushes, markers, and make whatever you want to with the power of your fingers. 

Paint boldly. Sketch freely. Create endlessly.

This is your chance to build that one idea that solves that problem you’ve been thinking about, with a chance to win a share of $100k.

‼️

Design matters now more than ever. This is your chance to design a solution to a problem, to test out new tools and use them to design and build something that makes a difference. We want to see how you use Figma to build something with purpose and meaning, something that improves your life, your community, or the world.
include a video walking through the work and the idea behind it, sharing the problem you are solving with your build, and the workflow used to bring the idea to life.
be made using Figma’s suite of products (Figma Make, MCP, agent, etc.) See the Tools section below for more info.
Must include link(s) to your project (live project link and community or working project file link)
Check out the last Makeathon’s winners here
Sharing to the Figma community receives bonus points and could be featured at the top of the
The winning submissions will be selected based on one or more of the following criteria:
Quality of work across design, build, impact, and craft.
Quality of the idea and its ability to solve a real problem for yourself, your community or the world.
Quality of the video showcasing your workflow, the design decisions behind the project, and steps others could take to emulate their process.
Building in Figma in a novel, innovative or unexpected way across Make, MCP, Local, Weave, design agent, etc.
5 points for projects shared on social
5 points for Figma projects share to the Figma Community

All participants get access to Figma Pro for the duration of the Config Makeathon, including 10k AI credits.
After joining, check your confirmation email from Contra and click the access code link to upgrade a free Starter team to a Professional team for free.
1,500 Figma Weave AI credits for new signups completed before June 11 at 5pm PST
The first 10k people who preregister before June 3 at 5pm PST will get access to the Beta of Figma design agent*.
After joining, check your confirmation email from Contra and click the access code link to upgrade a free Starter team to a Professional team and to sign up for a Figma Weave account.
*Note: Agent beta access is not guaranteed for the duration of the event and may be revoked without notice

This guide is your starting point for building with Figma's latest products. Whether you're a designer, developer, creative, or starting your own company, each of these tools is designed to help you build faster, ship smarter, and push the boundaries of what's possible during the Config Makeathon.
This guide covers five products you can use and explore:
— Prompt to code, directly from your design
— Overview and live examples from the community
Bring Figma library styles into Make
— How to use your design system as context
— Connect your Make app to real data
— Community examples of what people are building with Make
— Connect Figma to your favorite AI coding tools
— Full product overview and use case walkthroughs
Work with the AI agent in Figma Design (Help Center)
— How third-party agents can work with the Figma canvas
— AI-powered visual workflows for image, video, and creative media
— Real creative workflow examples from the Figma team
— An AI agent built into the Figma canvas
(beta access for preregistration before June 3, 5pm PST
— Full product overview and use case walkthroughs
Work with the AI agent in Figma Design (Help Center)
— How third-party agents can work with the Figma canvas

*If you worked on your submission with another user, you must tag that user in your entry. Teams are limited to two people. Only one person per team can submit, and that person is the sole recipient of any prize winnings if the submission is chosen. Both users get credit for the work, but only the posting user is entitled to the prize. Neither Figma nor Contra is responsible for splitting prizes between teammates, so you'll need to sort that out yourselves.
Best overall. Exceptional across design, build, impact, and craft.
Delivered on all dimensions. Creative use of Figma to ship a standout working prototype.
Not just what you built but how you built it. The most inventive design-to-build workflow using Make, MCP, Local, Weave, design agents, or beyond.
Most exemplary use of design to solve real problems for real people.
Screencasts, breakdowns, explainers—the most generous documentation of your process for the community to learn from.
The project that makes people stop scrolling.
Winners of the Config Makeathon will be announced June 23rd at

. Once you submit, you will be prompted to share your social links on Contra.
Note: this is required to qualify for the prize pool

is a great tool for this)

Tag the tool you built with

You’ve got 2 weeks to build something incredible, show off your Figma skills, and compete for a share of $100,000 in prizes.
Whether you're building products, tools, experiences, or something completely unexpected, now’s the time to ship.
Bearify looks amazing! Best of luck with the Figma Config Makeathon! 🐻🚀

webPageMetadata.title: All-in-One Life-Improving App - Config Makeathon Submission
webPageMetadata.description: Connect with next-gen talent and tools to get work underway. Hire more independents. Start more projects. Get more creative.

content.text: Really excited to be growing on Contra. 🚀
I've always loved platforms where creators can turn ideas into reality, showcase their imagination, and build something people genuinely enjoy using.
That's why I decided to join the
Config Makeathon and create something ambitious:
🐻 Bearify — an all-in-one companion app designed to simplify everyday life.
Instead of juggling multiple apps, Bearify brings everything together in one place:

• 📔 Personal Diary
• ⏰ Reminders
• 💪 Exercise Tracking
• ✨ Daily Quotes & Motivation
• 📚 Articles & Knowledge Hub
• 🎮 Interactive Pet Gaming
• 💬 Chat with Your Pet
• 🛍️ Shop Features
• ✅ Task Management
• 🛒 Grocery Planning
• ...and much more.
The goal was simple: create one app that helps users stay productive, motivated, organized, and entertained without constantly switching between different platforms.
Building this for the Config Makeathon was an incredible experience, and I'd love to hear what you think.
If you like the project, feel free to check it out and support it:
Feedback, thoughts, and support are always appreciated. ❤️
You did an amazing job. Good luck today! Keep inspiring 'n shining 'n energising others 🔥

Who else is planning to go? Or has anyone been to a Watch Party before? 👀
Also, Config starts today, and the #ConfigMakeathon results will be announced. I’m so nervous, fingers crossed! 🤞

Aww, thank you so much! 🥹🌸 Honestly, that’s probably why I ended up making this project, I was obsessed with all kinds of journals as a kid. I had friendship questionnaires, sketch journals, and entire albums dedicated to collecting stickers 😄 So I guess this project is a little love letter to that part of my childhood. And thank you for cheering me on today! I’m definitely excited (and a little nervous)! ✨

content.text: Day 5 — the final day of my journal posting challenge 💙
Over the past few days, I’ve shared little pieces of my life through this journal: exciting moments, anxious moments, memories, reflections, and even a very important zucchini update 🥒✨

When I started building this project, I wanted to create a space that would make self-reflection feel a little easier and a little more enjoyable. I hope both this product and my own experience have inspired at least a few people to try journaling, pay attention to their thoughts, and build a habit of reflecting on their days.
Today I’m waiting for the Makeathon results, and honestly, it feels both exciting and terrifying at the same time. 🤞
If you’d like to explore the project, try the product, or support my submission, you can find everything at the link below:
Thank you for all the likes, comments, shares, feedback, and kind words throughout this journey. Your support has meant more than you know. 💙
As someone who's capable of opening the same analytics page 37 times a day, I refuse to judge 😄
What I find interesting is how quickly you've grown in less than two months. Was there a moment when you noticed a clear shift in traction, or did it feel more like a slow accumulation of small wins?
Thanks, Rada 🫂 I can say that the algorithm loves you if you post every day, it is the most important thing :)

content.text: 👀 What do you do when you get a hyperfixation, and suddenly all you do is refresh Contra, check notifications, and obsess over engagement on your Makeathon submission?  Anyone else like this? 😅😅😅
Honestly, you might have noticed I’ve been a bit quiet with my growth updates lately — and that’s because for the past 2 weeks I was fully locked in on the #ConfigMakeathon 🥵
(I’ll also attach a screen recording in this post showing how to use the Skill Matrix for anyone who’s interested)
But today I’m back with a quick update to distract myself a little
𝐃𝐚𝐲 𝟓𝟐 🎉 𝐨𝐟 𝐦𝐲 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐠𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐜𝐡 𝟏,𝟎𝟎𝟎 𝐟𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐬

💎 +60 followers
💎 I made it into the Top 5 creators on Contra
💎 Discovery score grew by 4 points — now it’s 127
💎 Ben Huffman followed me — which honestly feels like a huge milestone for me, especially since I’ve been on Contra for less than 2 months
𝗪𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗜 𝗧𝗘𝗦𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗦𝗜𝗡𝗖𝗘 𝗠𝗬 𝗟𝗔𝗦𝗧 𝗣𝗢𝗦𝗧:
I’ll share everything at the end of the week 👀

Follow along if you’re curious to see whether it’s possible to grow on Contra from zero.

If you like my work please support my submission:
I’ve never won contests and these time also, but I really won so much love from people 🥹 Thank you guys! 🫶🫶🫶🫶
And congratulations to winners!!!! My personal fav - Doodle Fonts 😍

content.text: Day 4 of my personal journal posting challenge 🌿
One thing I’ve learned while building this project is that journaling doesn’t have to look the same for everyone.

Some people practice gratitude and focus on the good things that happened today. Others use expressive journaling to untangle emotions, reflective journaling to process experiences, or morning pages to clear their minds before the day begins.

You can try journaling and support my project here:
Thank you for following along and sharing your own journal moments with me 💙

Thank you Ester, for your support 😍 🙌  I appreciate it so much 🎉  We don't know each other, but you support me so much that I feel like you're my best friend every time I see your profile picture on Contra 😍

Skill Assessment Matrix is a SaaS designed to help ...

content.text: ⛳️ 𝗦𝗸𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗠𝗮𝘁𝗿𝗶𝘅 & 𝗥𝗼𝗮𝗱𝗺𝗮𝗽 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗗𝗲𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀

Skill Assessment Matrix is a SaaS designed to help Product Designers and UI/UX Designers better understand their current level, identify their strongest skills, and build a personalized growth roadmap
The idea came from a problem I experienced early in my career:
"𝙃𝙤𝙬 𝙙𝙤 𝙄 𝙠𝙣𝙤𝙬 𝙞𝙛 𝙄'𝙢 𝙖 𝙅𝙪𝙣𝙞𝙤𝙧, 𝙈𝙞𝙙-𝙡𝙚𝙫𝙚𝙡, 𝙤𝙧 𝙎𝙚𝙣𝙞𝙤𝙧 𝙙𝙚𝙨𝙞𝙜𝙣𝙚𝙧?"
This first version is focused on the desktop experience and was designed as the foundation for a larger SaaS product

𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗕𝗟𝗘𝗠
Designers often struggle with:
✦ Understanding their current level
✦ Identifying their strongest skills
✦ Knowing what to improve next
✦ Creating a clear career growth plan

𝗦𝗢𝗟𝗨𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡
Skill Assessment Matrix helps designers answer three simple questions:
✦ Where am I today?
✦ What are my strongest skills?
✦ What should I focus on next?
The product guides users through a complete career planning flow:
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟭 [Career Goals Setup] Users define their career goals and desired direction
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟮 [Self Assessment] Users evaluate their current skills across multiple competency areas
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟯 [Summary] The system generates a personalized overview highlighting strengths and growth opportunities.
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟰 [Roadmap Builder] Users receive a personalized roadmap and can further customize it by adding their own skills
𝗦𝘁𝗲𝗽 𝟱 [PDF Export] The final roadmap can be exported as a PDF and used as a practical career development plan.
⚡️This roadmap generation and export flow became one of the most valuable features of the project⚡️

𝗥𝗘𝗦𝗘𝗔𝗥𝗖𝗛 𝗙𝗢𝗨𝗡𝗗𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡
This project is based on more than 5 years of research into Skill assessment frameworks. The primary foundation for this work was the Figma Community Skills Matrix documentation, supported by dozens of additional public frameworks

𝗪𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗪𝗔𝗦 𝗧𝗘𝗦𝗧𝗘𝗗
✦ Figma AI Agent for generating new interfaces
✦ Figma Make for transforming designs into interactive product flows
✦ Combining a custom design system with generated product experiences
✦ Building complex multi-step user journeys inside Figma Make
✦ Creating SaaS-style product logic without traditional development workflows

𝗧𝗢𝗢𝗟𝗦 𝗨𝗦𝗘𝗗
Figma ✦ Figma AI Agent ✦ Figma Make ✦ MCP Servers
🎥 Watch the video to see the full design process and how the project was built
for contributing to the creative process, design discussions, design, brainstorming sessions, and product thinking throughout the project.

𝗦𝗨𝗣𝗣𝗢𝗥𝗧 (っ◔◡◔)っ

content.text: In a busy world filled with work, responsibilities, and constant distractions, many people lose touch with their emotions, needs, and personal goals
While journaling can improve self-awareness and well-being, traditional journals are not always practical to use every day
I created Digital Journal that makes self-reflection simple and accessible

💎 Track mood and energy
💎 Reflect on daily experiences
💎 Practice gratitude
💎 Capture thoughts, memories, and dreams
💎 Build greater self-awareness over time
The goal was to combine the convenience of digital tools with the warmth of a scrapbook-style journal
During the Makeathon, I explored and tested:

✔️Claude + Figma MCP for generating initial wireframes
✔️Figma AI Agent for early concept exploration and wireframing
✔️Figma for core pages visual design
✔️Figma Make for building and testing the interactive prototype
🎥 Watch the demo video to see the full design process, tools used, and how the project was built from idea to interactive prototype
If you enjoyed this project, I would appreciate your support ❤️

Super happy to share my entry for the Figma Makeathon 😊

Connected Earth. 

Language separates us. Sound connects us.

https://www.connected.earth

Connected Earth started with a simple observation: despite being more connected than ever, many of us feel increasingly disconnected from each other, our surroundings, and the places we live.

Most platforms ask us to compete for attention. More followers. More likes. More visibility. More noise. I wanted to explore the opposite.

Connected Earth is a living archive of sounds contributed by people around the world. A quiet place where someone can capture a moment from their corner of the planet and share it with someone they’ve never met.

The sound of rain in Scotland.
A train arriving in Tokyo.
Waves breaking in Australia.
Bikes whizzing past in park in Amsterdam.
Children playing on a street somewhere you’ll probably never visit.

None of these moments need translating. They’re immediately human.

Every recording becomes part of a growing global collection that can be explored individually or experienced through Earth Radio, a continuously evolving stream shaped entirely by community contributions. The more people contribute, the richer and more diverse the experience becomes.

Alongside the archive, Connected Earth includes Ambient Radio for creating evolving soundscapes from community recordings where sounds are stretched, overlayed, reversed, and can have user effects applied. We also have Resonance Tables for discovering related sounds and places, and Resonance Chains that allow recordings to be connected (chained) together into journeys spanning countries, cultures, and time zones. Together they transform individual moments into something larger than a collection of uploads.

Rather than likes, Connected Earth uses Resonations. Small acknowledgements that a sound resonated with someone else. No user account, No follower counts. No popularity contests. No pressure to perform. Just people sharing moments.

For those looking for calm, Ambient Mode transforms community recordings into evolving soundscapes that can be used for focus, reflection, relaxation, or simply feeling connected to somewhere else in the world.

The result is less of a social network and more of a shared listening experience.

The project was designed and built using Figma’s evolving ecosystem, including Figma Agents (Conversation & Moods), Make, AI-assisted (ChatGPT > validation) workflows, and iterative prototyping. What began as a conversation with design tools quickly became an exploration of how technology might help us feel a little more connected to one another.

Connected Earth is fully desktop and mobile compatible, allowing people to contribute, discover, listen, and explore wherever they happen to be.

In a world increasingly driven by content, Connected Earth is an experiment in presence. A reminder that while we may speak different languages, we often experience the same rain, the same wind, the same birdsong, and the same quiet.

And perhaps that matters more than we think.
happiness is much simpler than we think... it is soooooo true! thanks for this reminder

portfolioProject.description:
I built a virtual Ikebana experience. It's called Project: Ikebana. 🌸
It's the first time I've ever built something with gesture controls, worked with 3D as...

content.text: I built a virtual Ikebana experience. It's called Project: Ikebana. 🌸
It's the first time I've ever built something with gesture controls, worked with 3D assets, and vibecoded this much with Figma Make.
Ikebana is the traditional Japanese art of flower arranging. It literally translates to "giving life to flowers."
Project: Ikebana is my attempt to bring that idea into a digital space.
You can create floral arrangements in both 2D and 3D, using either hand gestures or traditional mouse controls.
The part I love most: the little moments of whimsy.

This project started with a bunch of moodboarding into FigJam board, grew through 100+ make iterations, and somehow led me into learning 3D workflows, gesture interactions, databases, debugging, and a whole lot more.
I used Weave to create many of the visual assets and models, then brought everything together in Figma Make with Supabase powering the project's content library.
I love how tools like Weave and Make encourage you to focus on what something should feel like, not just how to build it.
If there's one thing I'd love people to take away from this project:
